figures.caption: Fig. 5: Case study for Scenario S1 and its task-planning results. S2 and S3 extend this setup with more robots, parts, and rules. (a) Setup for S1, where two robots assemble a small gear (SG) and a medium circular pin (MCP). (b) Task-planning automata that shows safety violations. (c) Repaired automata, where the safety constraints are enforced. I , M , P , T , and C denote idle, move-to-pick, pick, transport, and place states, respectively. x, y, z, and π denote the corresponding events.
Fig. 2: Process planning workflow. NL product requirements are transformed into structured requirements using an LLM, generating a task plan in DAG, and compiling this DAG into a FSA plan.

Fig. 4: CCA safety validation architecture. The task plan is traversed with the safety automata using DFS-based validation to detect violations. Safety violations are accumulated and returned as structured feedback for replanning.
TABLE I: Representative examples of the feedback prompt.
